title: To bring Yui back
wikitext:
[]: To bring Yui back.             Quote Post by Zephros » Tue Mar 09, 2010 6:26 pm Just thought of something interesting while posting to the soul/mind debate. To construct Shinji, they needed his body. They had that and his soul floating around Unit -O1 entry plug. So how was Gendo going to reconstruct Yui without her body? This maybe obvious for some here, but it was new to me. I think before hand, they were working on a Human/Angel hybrid, since I think at the time they were considering different options for pilots. Then Yui up and does her disappearing act and Gendo goes over the bend with loss. A few days later he comes up with a great idea to get Yui back to him. He takes her 'remains' and uses them with the hybrid, intending on using the Hybrid to trigger his own Instrumentality. To bring Yui back he has the blueprints of her in Rei and her soul in Unit 1. Result, Yui reborn. Makes sense? Any dissenters?

[]:             Quote Post by Uriel Septim VII » Tue Mar 09, 2010 6:32 pm I saw it being that Gendo wanted to initiate his own personal Instrumentality so that his and Yui's souls would be united forever. Unless I missed an important line or misinterpreted it, that's what it seemed to be. So the objective wasn't to recreate Yui in a body that resembled her own.

[]: Re: To bring Yui back.             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Mar 09, 2010 6:58 pm Zephros wrote: So how was Gendo going to reconstruct Yui without her body? This maybe obvious for some here, but it was new to me.

[]:             Quote Post by Zephros » Tue Mar 09, 2010 10:47 pm Also note that the radio during this car ride mentions the "oral stage" of development, being dependent on your mother. So the main difference is that in Yui's case, she didn't want to leave, whereas with Shinji's case, he chose to leave. Another thing that's sometimes brought up is that Evas are kind of like an empty container without a soul, so there may be this affinity for a soul to stay there once the Contact Experiment is made. So Yui doesn't want to leave the Eva, the salvage operation ends up pulling a body out with a soul that isn't Yui (Lilith).

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Tue Mar 09, 2010 11:04 pm Zephros wrote: [One. How did Lilith's soul end up in Unit-01? Is there a scene I missed, or an obscure quote I don't know of?

[]:             Quote Post by Zephros » Wed Mar 10, 2010 1:14 am Shin-seiki wrote: Zephros wrote: [One. How did Lilith's soul end up in Unit-01? Is there a scene I missed, or an obscure quote I don't know of?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Mar 10, 2010 6:23 am Zephros wrote: Okay. I reiterate my question. Where does it say they got Lilith's Soul from Unit-01? I knew about the picture linking them, but no where in there does it say they got the soul from the contact experiment.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Wed Mar 10, 2010 9:20 am Reichu wrote: One of the reasons for this explanation is that it "takes advantage" of Eva-01 and Lilith apparently still being physically connected when the CE and perhaps the subsequent salvage took place.

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Wed Mar 10, 2010 11:30 am Shin-seiki wrote: I recall that MDWigs was a proponent of the idea that the salvage operation, while failing to get Yui back, did somehow snag Lilith's soul, with Rei as the (inadvertent?) result.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Wed Mar 10, 2010 11:46 am Fazmotron wrote: Would that mean the (full soul) Rei/Lilith would then have had a contact experiment with Unit-00 in which she lost part of her soul, that part becoming the resident soul in Unit-00? Has this theory been brought up before? (probably has)

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Mar 10, 2010 4:31 pm To address the original question, I'm not really sure that Gendou ever intended to physically "reconstruct" Yui, at least, not after the failure of the salvage operation. Indeed, it's not even clear that Gendou was behind the salvage operation. This ties into the point raised about how Rei was a product of the salvage operation, with Lilith being extracted instead of Yui, probably from the still linked mass of Unit-01 and Lilith. Firstly, Rei looks Like Yui, which is a pretty big hint that the salvage operation probably tried to form a Yui shaped "vessel" in order to draw her out. But because Yui didn't want to come their efforts failed no matter how hard they pulled, until eventually they pulled so hard that Lilith plopped out instead into the Yui "shaped" form of Rei. Anyway, this theory is perhaps supported by Fuyutsuki's words to Gendou in #23' Episode #23' wrote: FUYUTSUKI: Rei... She is the product of my despair and at the same time, the vessel of your hope even now.

[]:             Quote Post by Zephros » Wed Mar 10, 2010 5:47 pm @Reichu. Thank-you for clearing that up for me. I don't see how it was heavily implied, but I do see the logic of it now. @Obsessivemathsfreak(nice name btw), you win the thread here, not just by thoroughly explaining to me what happened, but also going farther and extrapolating what Gendo's motives were. Awesome. @Shin-seiki, I don't have access to the amount of corollary information most people on this forum seem to, almost it seems like you are taking that knowledge for granted. I only JUST read the wiki and learned about the FAR upon joining this forum. So, to me, Rei having Lilith's soul is a theory, since it was only implied in canon. (I'm also going from memory here, so a few quick lines may say other-wise.) But, considering you do have that knowledge, I'll take your word on it. Thx. To everyone else, thank-you for helping me. The idea was a random bit of logic that struck me, and I called upon the gurus here to help me dismiss or embrace it. Cheers!

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Mar 10, 2010 5:52 pm Uriel Septim VII wrote: I saw it being that Gendo wanted to initiate his own personal Instrumentality so that his and Yui's souls would be united forever. Unless I missed an important line or misinterpreted it, that's what it seemed to be.

[]: Re: To bring Yui back.             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Wed Mar 10, 2010 6:34 pm Ornette wrote: Zephros wrote: So how was Gendo going to reconstruct Yui without her body? This maybe obvious for some here, but it was new to me.

[]: Re: To bring Yui back.             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Mar 10, 2010 7:27 pm supershinjiasukashipper wrote: actually, she didn't come back because ritsuko's mother sabatoged it to get with gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Wed Mar 10, 2010 7:40 pm there was a quote from her though in that episode where fuyuski was kiddnapped, or one of those flashback episodes. mabey it was the same episode where Shinji was liquified in the entry plug, but i do remember a direct comment.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Mar 10, 2010 7:49 pm This: Episode 21 wrote: AKAGI (MONO): Those were Ms. Yui's final words. The irregular circumstances erased her from this world. Exactly as I had wished. I am such a horrible woman. Ritchan, Director Ikari changed after that day.

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Wed Mar 10, 2010 7:55 pm but the words "im such a horrible woman" denote guilt, meaning she was involved, and since she would be heading recovery operations, why would she undo her own work? on another note, it makes me happy we can have a conversation involving something other than my posting. thanks ornett.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Wed Mar 10, 2010 7:58 pm Not at all. Guilt can mean involvement, it can also just mean that she WANTED something to go wrong, and now that it has she feels horrible because it did. I think it's definitely just that she feels bad that things happened "exactly as [she] had wanted."

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Wed Mar 10, 2010 8:01 pm Legendary wrote: Not at all. Guilt can mean involvement, it can also just mean that she WANTED something to go wrong, and now that it has she feels horrible because it did. I think it's definitely just that she feels bad that things happened "exactly as [she] had wanted."
